title: Kenya County Budget Implementation Financial Year:2023/2024-Dashboard
description:
Project Overview
This is a comprehensive, interactive data dashboard I developed to visualize and analyze the budget performance of 47 counties in Kenya for the financial year 2023/24. The project transforms complex public financial data from the Office of the Controller of Budget into an accessible, interactive web application.

Key Features:
Interactive Map Visualization

-Choropleth map with color-coded counties based on key performance metrics

-Dual-layer visualization (polygon boundaries + circle markers) for flexible data exploration

-Popup information windows displaying detailed county-specific data

-Custom legend system adapting to different metrics

Multi-Metric Analysis:
The dashboard tracks six key performance indicators:

-Overall absorption rate

-Development absorption rate

-Revenue performance

-Wage bill as percentage of revenue

-Pending bills (in Kshs. Billion)

-Total budget allocation

Comprehensive Dashboard Tabs:

-Performance Analysis: Top-performing counties and budget allocation patterns

-County Comparison: Side-by-side radar chart comparison of any two counties

-Trend Analysis: Historical performance trends over five years

-Revenue Analysis: Revenue sources breakdown and own-source revenue leaders

-Efficiency Metrics: Scatter plot matrices showing absorption vs. revenue performance

Interactive Controls:

.Metric selector to change map coloring

Region and county filters

Year selector for trend analysis

Layer toggles for map visualization control

Data export functionality (CSV)

Technical Implementation
Frontend Technologies

Leaflet.js for interactive mapping with custom GeoJSON integration

Chart.js and D3.js for all data visualizations (bar charts, doughnut charts, scatter plots, radar charts, line graphs)

PapaParse for CSV data handling

HTML5/CSS3 with responsive design principles

Data Architecture

47 Kenyan counties with 15+ data points each

GeoJSON boundary data integrated with county-level metrics

Regional aggregation for comparative analysis

Historical trend data spanning 5 financial years

Key Insights Delivered
The dashboard reveals critical patterns in Kenya's county financial management:

Significant variation in budget absorption rates (64.7% to 90.8%)

Heavy dependency on national government transfers (72% of revenue)

Wage bill pressures exceeding legal limits in multiple counties

Development expenditure lagging at only 24.4% of total spending

Pending bills exceeding Kshs. 181 billion overall

Impact & Value
This tool serves multiple stakeholders:

Government officials can identify underperforming counties and best practices

Citizens gain transparency into public fund utilization

Researchers access clean, visualizable financial data

Policymakers can make data-driven resource allocation decisions
